headline: Howard Lindzon on Seed Investing: Why Most of Your Bets Will Fail: and Why That’s Okay
summary: Howard Lindzon does something most venture capitalists would never do: he tells his limited partners, upfront, that most of his bets will fail. The StockTwits CEO and Social Capital founder describes his seed fund using a metaphor he borrowed from a Larry David sensibility. “You are betting on me to hang 30 pieces of art ... Howard Lindzon on Seed Investing: Why Most of Your Bets Will Fail: and Why That’s Okay

headline: Kiplinger’s May 2026 Letter Says Yields From 3% to 13% Are Available Right Now Despite Iran War Uncertainty
summary: Kiplinger’s Personal Finance May 2026 letter makes it clear that if you are looking for yield, the door is wide open across the entire risk spectrum. We are seeing a massive spread, with opportunities ranging from stable 3% municipal bonds to 13% for those willing to get aggressive with business development companies. This is unfolding ... Kiplinger’s May 2026 Letter Says Yields From 3% to 13% Are Available Right Now Despite Iran War Uncertainty

headline: Is Charles Schwab (SCHW) Offering Opportunity After Recent Share Price Softness?
summary: Wondering if Charles Schwab at around US$90.88 is priced attractively or already fully reflecting expectations? This article walks through what the current share price may be implying about value. The stock is up 2.6% over the last week, has slipped 1.5% over the past month, is down 10.5% year to date, and has returned 3.8% over the last year, 83.5% over three years, and 34.6% over five years. Recent coverage has focused on Charles Schwab's role in the broader capital markets industry and...
